rag_rules: Use AGREE guarantees to express deterministic relations between visible symbols.
Keep contract expressions within valid component scope; avoid implementation-only paths unless they are validly exposed.
Use only legal AGREE scalar types: bool, int, and real.
Write expressions with AGREE logical and branching syntax, including and/or/not, =>, and if-then-else when needed.
generic_rules: Prefer direct guarantees for observable equality or dependency relations rather than introducing unnecessary helper definitions.
Only encode relations supported by visible identifiers and explicit model facts; avoid inventing thresholds, constants, or hidden variables.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE clause forms include assume "label": expr; guarantee "label": expr; eq name : type = expr; const name : type = value;
Boolean comparisons and equality relations produce bool expressions, so any helper defined from them should be typed bool.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL property names or unit tokens as if they were AGREE variables.
Do not use invalid type names such as boolean or integer, and do not create type mismatches between declared helpers and their expressions.
irrelevant_topics: Range-envelope and clamping patterns are not directly useful unless the requirement provides explicit bounds or limit behavior.
